NationalServicePersonnel.title: NationalServicePersonnel
NationalServicePersonnel.description: Represents a National Service Personnel record.

id.description: Unique identifier for the National Service Personnel record (System ID). Format: LDM0001, LDM0002, etc.

nssNumber.description: Unique service number assigned to the NSP by the National Service Secretariat (NSS Number).

fullName.description: Full name of the NSP (concatenation of surname and other names).

courseOfStudy.description: The course of study pursued by the NSP.

phone.description: Contact phone number of the NSP.

residentialAddress.description: Permanent residential address of the NSP.

gpsAddress.description: GPS address of the NSP's residence.

posting.description: The location or department where the NSP is posted (Place of service).

nextOfKinName.description: Name of the NSP's next of kin.

nextOfKinPhone.description: Contact number of the NSP's next of kin.

createdDate.description: The date the NSP record was created.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date the NSP record was last updated.

isDisabled.description: Indicates if the NSP record is disabled (soft delete).

serviceYear.description: The year the NSP is serving in.

Submission.title: Submission
Submission.description: Represents a monthly submission record for an NSP.

id.description: Unique identifier for the submission record.

nspId.description: Reference to the NationalServicePersonnel. (Relationship: NationalServicePersonnel 1:N Submission)

deskOfficerName.description: Name of the desk officer who marked the submission (optional).

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the system. Does not store authentication data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user (matches auth uid).

isAdmin.description: Indicates if the user has administrative privileges.

AuditLog.title: AuditLog
AuditLog.description: Represents an audit log entry for critical system actions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the audit log record.

adminId.description: The UID of the administrator who performed the action.

details.description: An object containing details about the action, e.g., { nspId: '...', nspName: '...' }.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of when the action occurred.

definition.description: Represents a district. Contains district metadata. Used for scoping users and NSPs.

params.name: districtId
params.description: The unique identifier of the district.

definition.description: Collection to store admin user flags. The existence of a document with isAdmin:true indicates admin rights.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (matches auth uid).

definition.description: Represents a user (Desk Officer or Admin) associated with a district.

params.name: districtId
params.description: The unique identifier of the district.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Represents a National Service Personnel record.

params.name: districtId
params.description: The unique identifier of the district.

params.name: personnelId
params.description: The unique identifier of the national service personnel.

definition.description: Represents a monthly submission record for an NSP.

params.name: districtId
params.description: The unique identifier of the district.

params.name: personnelId
params.description: The unique identifier of the national service personnel.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the submission.

definition.description: Collection to store audit logs for critical administrative actions.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique identifier of the log entry.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure is designed to support the NSP management and submission system. The structure is optimized for the core requirements of NSP record management, monthly submission tracking, and role-based authentication. The use of a top-level `admins` collection provides a simple and secure way to manage admin privileges without relying on custom claims. The `auditLogs` collection is added to track critical admin actions for security and accountability.
